methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $3.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
1X Technologies, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $123.5M to date, is valued here using a two-method triangulation against a peer set of 4 same-sector comparables at similar or adjacent stages (Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics), plus 1 public comparable and 8 private peers in the broader robotics ecosystem.

The stage-progression method, applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier to a $3.0B total-raised proxy, implies a valuation of $5.7B. The peer-median method, anchored to the four direct comparables, yields $1.6B, with the peer set ranging from $1.0B (Nimble Robotics, Series C) to $1.8B (Bedrock Robotics, Series B). These two approaches diverge materially, reflecting uncertainty around whether 1X's capital deployment trajectory and stage progression justify the upper end of the range or whether peer multiples more accurately reflect current market conditions in robotics.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $6.6B (high), with a median of $3.7B. The wide spread reflects dependency on method selection and the stage-progression model's sensitivity to forward-looking multiplier assumptions. Key uncertainties include the degree to which 1X's product maturity and go-to-market progress warrant a premium or discount relative to peers, and whether the $3.0B proxy itself accurately reflects intrinsic funding capacity.
